subagent_reports.topic: Discovery opportunity scout — missed leverage points for ADV tool-call latency optimization

sources.label: Context7 /isaacs/node-lru-cache — cache.fetch(key, {signal})
sources.summary: Canonical stale-while-revalidate pattern threads the AbortSignal INTO fetchMethod body (fetch(url, {signal})) so underlying I/O is actually cancelled on timeout. With ignoreFetchAbort:true the caller gets control back but the fetch body keeps running unless signal is propagated.

sources.label: ADV probe closures ignore context.signal
sources.summary: fetch closures call getTemporalHealth/getWorktreeCensus/scanSnapshotHealth WITHOUT passing context.signal. ProbeCacheFetchContext exposes signal but it is dropped, so 2s/10s timeouts return stale control while the underlying work runs unbounded.

sources.label: Prep doc HIGH findings — view-lazy status + summary read model
sources.summary: summary view still builds full status payload before projection (anti-pattern: applyStatusView projects after full build, status.ts:847-850). listResolvedChanges unions memo+visibility+disk+archive then hydrates every change; status re-enriches each recent change.

sources.label: Visibility project-attribute inconsistency
sources.summary: Backlog claim queries use AdvAffectedProjects; change enumeration uses AdvProjectId (documented as pre-existing inconsistency). Attribute drift forces slow disk fallback on hot Visibility paths.

sources.label: adv_run_test best-effort recording + no perf budget guard
sources.summary: testRunRecordedSignal awaited best-effort in try/catch; silent swallow on failure. Only synthetic listChangeWorkflowIds p99 guard exists; no tool-level p50/p95 budget for adv_status/adv_change_list/adv_change_show.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Macro-architecture is SOUND and already validated by prep doc against Context7 (Visibility-first, lru-cache stale-while-revalidate, signal/query-only surface, Zod preservation). The prep correctly classifies status view projection as the one ANTI-PATTERN (project-after-build). Scouting surfaces FOUR under-specified leverage points the prep findings imply but do not isolate as discrete wins: (1) probe AbortSignal is created but never threaded into fetch bodies, so timeouts bound the CALLER not the WORK — a near-free reliability+latency win that directly serves the proposal's 'fewer wrapper timeouts' outcome; (2) the summary read-model and view-lazy execution are TWO separable wins (lazy probe gating vs. summary-index hydration) that should be sequenced, lazy-first (low risk) then index (higher risk); (3) the AdvProjectId/AdvAffectedProjects drift is a latent fast-path failure that should be fixed BEFORE summary-index work depends on Visibility, else the index inherits a flaky fast path; (4) adv_run_test degraded-recording must become a structural status field, not a swallowed catch, to honor the 'must not silently drop evidence' constraint. All boring, proven fixes — no novel infra.

validation.notes: No contract/security/release blocker. All candidates stay inside approved In-Scope and respect the Must-Not list (no stale-as-authority, no fireSignalAndRefresh bypass, no silent evidence drop, no heuristic correctness). Candidates are sequencing/decomposition guidance, not new product commitments.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt 4 leverage points as discovery-scoped work items, sequenced to de-risk: (1) FIRST fix Visibility project-attribute drift (structural prerequisite for any Visibility-backed summary read model); (2) thread probe context.signal into getTemporalHealth/getWorktreeCensus/scanSnapshotHealth/computeSearchAttributesSnapshot fetch bodies (near-free, bounds real work, serves 'fewer timeouts'); (3) split status optimization into lazy-probe-gating (adopt_now, low risk) then summary-index hydration (design_around, surface SLO tradeoff to user); (4) convert adv_run_test best-effort catch into an explicit degraded-recording status field surfaced in output. Defer live-Temporal benchmark mode and per-tool wall-time telemetry to acceptance-evidence tasks — needed to PROVE wins but not themselves latency wins. Surface to user: the summary-index hydration boundary (which fields are mandatory in default lightweight output) is the one genuine user-value tradeoff requiring a decision.
follow_ups: Probe signal-threading needs tests for both stale-cache-hit and cold-fetch-timeout cases to prove the underlying work is actually cancelled (cite lru-cache ignoreFetchAbort semantics).
AdvProjectId vs AdvAffectedProjects fix requires a search-attribute registration/migration test so the fast path is structurally reliable before index work depends on it.
adv_run_test degraded-recording status field touches the durable-evidence Must-Not constraint — confirm spec home (advance-workflow vs advance-meta) during design.
No SLO thresholds (p50/p95) exist yet; perf-budget tests cannot be written until discovery sets targets — this is a DONE_WHEN dependency for the testing finding.

subagent_reports.topic: Design-phase leverage scout — high-payoff/low-risk wins within approved optimizeAdvToolCalls design scope

sources.label: Context7 /isaacs/node-lru-cache — cache.fetch(key,{signal}) + ignoreFetchAbort
sources.summary: Canonical stale-while-revalidate threads AbortSignal INTO fetchMethod body (fetch(url,{signal})). With ignoreFetchAbort:true + allowStaleOnFetchAbort:true the CALLER regains control on timeout but the underlying fetch keeps running unless signal is propagated into the I/O. Bounding the caller is not bounding the work.

sources.label: ADV probe fetch closures drop context.signal
sources.summary: ProbeCacheFetchContext exposes signal and fetchMethod forwards it, but every status probe closure (getTemporalHealth/getWorktreeCensus/computeSearchAttributesSnapshot/scanSnapshotHealth) ignores it. 2s/10s timeouts return stale control while real work runs unbounded — confirms KD4 is currently unimplemented.

sources.label: Status full-load-before-view + sequential recent-change enrichment
sources.summary: loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry runs before view branching; recentChangeEnrichment loops enrichRecentChangeStatus sequentially with per-change awaits. Plan gating (plan.temporalHealth/queueServiceability/worktreeCleanup) already exists at 1549/1568/1714 — view-lazy scaffold is partly present; the unguarded full statusLoad + serial enrichment are the residual costs.

sources.label: Visibility project-scope already converged on AdvAffectedProjects
sources.summary: Hot query path filters registered AdvAffectedProjects KeywordList (rq-visibilityProjectScope01). AdvProjectId is NOT in ADV_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES; ADVANCE_TEMPORAL_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES.projectId='AdvProjectId' (contracts.ts:34) is a legacy/dead constant. KD3 'fix drift first' is largely already satisfied — residual is stale-constant cleanup, not a blocking prerequisite.

sources.label: adv_run_test best-effort recording catch swallows signal failure
sources.summary: testRunRecordedSignal awaited inside try/catch{} that swallows all failures; formatToolOutput return (508+) has command result + evidence{} but NO recording-status field. KD4/AC5 require a distinct structural recording-status field; runId is already computed (442) and threaded (537), so the field has a natural carrier.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is sound and boring — Visibility-first reads, lru-cache stale-while-revalidate, view planner, typed degraded states, fail-closed bench. Two design assumptions are now PARTLY OVERTAKEN BY THE CODE and should be re-scoped before planning: (1) KD3's 'fix AdvProjectId/AdvAffectedProjects drift before depending on fast paths' is largely DONE — list-change-workflows already filters the registered AdvAffectedProjects and AdvProjectId is unregistered/dead; the residual is a stale-constant cleanup (contracts.ts:34), so KD3 should be DOWNGRADED from prerequisite slice to a small cleanup task, not gating summary-index work. (2) The view-lazy scaffold partly EXISTS (plan.* gating at status.ts:1549/1568/1714, migrationStatus already view-gated at 1536) — so KD1's win narrows to two concrete residuals: the unguarded loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry full load (1531-1535) and the sequential recentChangeEnrichment loop (1686-1707). The single confirmed near-free reliability+latency win is KD4 probe signal-threading: signal is created and forwarded but dropped by every probe closure, so timeouts bound the caller not the work — exactly the 'fewer wrapper timeouts' outcome, fixable without new infra. adv_run_test (KD4 recording status) is a structural-correctness fix (swap swallowed catch for a typed recording-status field carried alongside the existing runId), directly serving AC5/DONT7.

validation.notes: No contract/security/release blocker. All candidates stay inside approved In-Scope and respect Must-Not (C7/DONT5 stale-not-authority, DONT6 fireSignalAndRefresh, DONT7 no silent evidence drop, DONT8 no heuristic correctness). Candidates are sequencing/decomposition guidance, not new product commitments. Two material corrections to prior design assumptions (KD3 already mostly done; view-lazy scaffold partly present) reduce risk and scope — they do not contradict the design's direction.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Sequence design tasks to bank the near-free wins first and de-risk the index work: (1) KD4 probe signal-threading — thread context.signal into getTemporalHealth/getWorktreeCensus/computeSearchAttributesSnapshot/scanSnapshotHealth where the adapter accepts AbortSignal, else classify non-cancellable and exclude from summary; tests must prove the underlying work is actually cancelled on cold-fetch-timeout (cite lru-cache ignoreFetchAbort semantics), not just that the caller returns. (2) adv_run_test recording-status field — replace the swallowed catch (test.ts:475-477) with a typed degraded/ok/timed-out recording status carried beside the existing runId in formatToolOutput; bounded ≤300ms wait per design. (3) KD1 residuals only — gate loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry behind the summary plan (or a lighter summary loader) and parallelize/bound recentChangeEnrichment; the plan.* gating scaffold already exists, so this is targeted, not a rewrite. (4) DOWNGRADE KD3 to a stale-constant cleanup (remove/realign contracts.ts:34 AdvProjectId) — it is NOT a blocking prerequisite because the hot path already uses AdvAffectedProjects; do not let it gate summary-index work. USER TRADEOFF requiring a decision: the summary-plan mandatory field set (which orientation fields stay in lean default vs move to health/hygiene) — this is the one genuine user-value boundary (AC1/AC2) and should be surfaced explicitly, not inferred.
follow_ups: WARNING: prompt VERIFICATION block present but design omits SLO p50/p95 probe-thread targets; SC2 sets tool-level p95 (summary ≤2s, list ≤1s, run_test overhead ≤500ms) but no per-probe budget — perf tests for KD4 must assert cancellation behavior, not just latency, until probe-level targets exist.

KD3 downgrade depends on confirming no OTHER reader still queries the unregistered AdvProjectId — observability.test.ts:49 and contracts.ts:34 reference it; verify these are assertions/legacy-only before removing.

adv_run_test recording-status field touches durable-evidence Must-Not (DONT7/AC5); confirm spec home (advance-workflow vs advance-meta) and that the field is structural (typed enum) not prose during planning.
Live-Temporal bench mode (AC6) and per-tool wall-time telemetry are acceptance-EVIDENCE tasks needed to PROVE wins, not themselves latency wins — keep them sequenced after the four leverage fixes.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation — Optimize ADV tool calls

sources.label: lru-cache cache.fetch(key,{signal}) canonical docs (Context7 /isaacs/node-lru-cache)
sources.summary: Confirms KD4: fetchMethod receives (key, staleValue, {signal}); signal must be threaded into the underlying fetch body to actually cancel work. ignoreFetchAbort:true returns caller control while background work continues unless signal is propagated. allowStale/allowStaleOnFetchAbort/allowStaleOnFetchRejection give stale-on-abort + coalescing. Validates design's probe semantics direction.

sources.label: probe-cache.ts current implementation
sources.summary: createProbeCache ALREADY threads signal into options.fetch(key,{signal,staleValue}) and sets allowStale/allowStaleOnFetchAbort/allowStaleOnFetchRejection/ignoreFetchAbort. The cancellation gap is NOT in probe-cache.ts — design's KD4 rationale citing probe-cache.ts:73-78,94-102 as the drop site is imprecise; the drop is in status.ts closures.

sources.label: status.ts probe closures drop context.signal
sources.summary: Confirmed: probe fetch closures take only key/root and call getTemporalHealth/getWorktreeCensus/scanSnapshotHealth WITHOUT passing context.signal, so timeouts bound caller not work. KD4's structural fix target is accurate; slice B scoping (thread signal OR classify non-cancellable) is correct.

sources.label: status.ts unguarded summary load + existing view-plan scaffold
sources.summary: loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry runs unconditionally for all views (including summary); migrationStatus already gated to health/hygiene; buildStatusViewPlan(view) scaffold already present. Validates KD1 'finish residuals, not rewrite'.

sources.label: test.ts silent best-effort recording catch
sources.summary: Confirmed: testRunRecordedSignal awaited inside try with empty catch {} comment 'non-fatal if signal fire fails'. Validates KD5 problem statement and AC5/DONT7 motivation for typed recording-status union.

sources.label: bench-adv-latency.ts disk/temporal mode + fail-closed
sources.summary: Confirmed: disk mode documented as not measuring Temporal RTT; --mode temporal throws fail-closed refusing to fake a bundle. Validates KD6 and AC6; design extends labeling rather than inventing fail-closed behavior.

sources.label: Spec advance-meta rq-statusProbeCache01 (warrant for AC3)
sources.summary: Existing law: bounded cached probes must surface _freshness (cached_at, stale, error); stale probe data may inform recommendations but MUST NOT authorize safety-critical mutations (reclaim/restart/override/unlock/archive). Design KD2/KD3/KD4 are consistent — no contradiction; spec delta to add probe cancellation/degraded semantics is compatible.

sources.label: Spec backlog-coordination rq-statusCliWorkerFree01 + rq-backlogCoord05 (warrant for AC4)
sources.summary: Existing law: status rows built from Visibility search attributes worker-free, fail closed on Temporal failure, no per-change getState for default table; rq-backlogCoord05 mandates O(1) Visibility IN-query for active-change annotation. Design KD2/KD3 (summary/Visibility for orientation only; authoritative paths keep full state) is consistent and reinforces this spec; no conflict.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is architecturally sound and boring-by-the-book. It optimizes hot read paths via view-specific planning, typed degraded states, and Visibility/summary read models while preserving Temporal as authoritative — directly aligned with existing laws rq-statusProbeCache01, rq-statusCliWorkerFree01, rq-dashboardWorkerFree01, and rq-backlogCoord05. All seven source citations the design relies on were verified accurate against the live tree, and external lru-cache semantics confirm the probe-cancellation direction. KD1 (finish residuals, not rewrite) is correct: buildStatusViewPlan scaffold and migrationStatus view-gating already exist; loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry is genuinely unguarded for summary. KD5/KD6 problem statements match the live silent-catch and disk/temporal-mode code. One precision defect: KD4's rationale attributes the signal-drop to probe-cache.ts:73-78,94-102, but probe-cache.ts ALREADY threads signal correctly; the actual drop is in the status.ts:200-211,241-245 closures (which the design also cites). The fix target and slice B scoping remain correct, so this is a documentation-precision caution, not a design error. No simpler equivalent approach overlooked — the design already rejects rewrite in favor of residual cleanup and uses proven lru-cache stale-while-revalidate rather than novel infra. No spec conflict found; KD7 commits to spec deltas (advance-meta primary, tdd-contract only if evidence semantics change, backlog-coordination only if live Visibility behavior changes), which matches warrant homes.

validation.notes: VALIDATED. All four validation dimensions pass with sourced evidence. CORRECTNESS: design solves all six objectives; AC1-AC8 map to verified code/spec anchors. SIMPLICITY: design explicitly chooses residual cleanup over rewrite and reuses existing probe-cache stale-while-revalidate + existing fail-closed bench mode; no materially simpler equivalent overlooked. SPEC-LAW COMPLIANCE: no contradiction with rq-statusProbeCache01, rq-statusCliWorkerFree01, rq-dashboardWorkerFree01, rq-backlogCoord05; KD2/KD3 reinforce rather than violate the worker-free/authoritative-state boundary. KEY ALTERNATIVES: none significant overlooked. One non-blocking caution: KD4 rationale should correct the signal-drop attribution from probe-cache.ts to the status.ts probe closures (probe-cache.ts already threads signal); fix target unaffected. DDC3's 300ms recording wait and DDC4 benchmark fields are reasonable and testable per AC7.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Approve the design (VERDICT: VALIDATED). It is correct, simpler-by-the-book, and spec-compliant, with all material claims verified against live source and canonical lru-cache docs. Before or during execution, tighten KD4's rationale text: the AbortSignal is already threaded inside probe-cache.ts (createProbeCache passes {signal,staleValue} into options.fetch and sets allowStaleOnFetchAbort/ignoreFetchAbort); the real cancellation gap is the status.ts:200-211,241-245 probe closures that accept the context but never forward context.signal into getTemporalHealth/getWorktreeCensus/scanSnapshotHealth. Slice B should therefore (a) forward context.signal from those closures into operations that accept it, and (b) classify scanSnapshotHealth/computeSearchAttributesSnapshot as non-cancellable and exclude them from summary if they cannot honor the signal — both already implied by the design. Keep KD7 spec deltas anchored to advance-meta (probe/status/benchmark) with tdd-contract touched only if adv_run_test recording changes TDD evidence semantics. No agenda items required; no blockers.

documents.proposal:
# Optimize ADV tool calls

## Why

Advance tool calls are slower than needed on common agent paths, especially `adv_status`, status-backed change hydration, `adv_run_test` evidence recording, and latency benchmarking. A read-only Tron scan found concrete hotspots and risks across status, Temporal storage, probe caching, roadmap/backlog annotation, test execution recording, and the latency harness.

## Problem Statement

Advance tool calls are slower than needed on common agent paths, especially `adv_status`, status-backed change hydration, `adv_run_test` evidence recording, and latency benchmarking. We need optimize these paths without weakening reliability guarantees: Temporal correctness, cache invalidation, diagnostic safety, durable evidence, and spec-law behavior must remain structural and verified.

## What Changes

Investigate and handle all evidence-backed Tron findings from the speed reconnaissance. The change will improve hot-path latency where safely possible, preserve structural correctness and reliability guarantees, and produce measurable evidence for latency claims.

The work is expected to cover:

- `adv_status` summary/read-model behavior and large-WIP performance.
- Status probe caching, timeout, and cancellation boundaries.
- `adv_run_test` post-command evidence-recording latency and degraded-recording semantics.
- Live Temporal latency benchmarking, not only disk-substitute benchmarking.
- Change list/status hydration, cache/memo behavior, and Visibility-backed read paths.
- Roadmap/backlog annotation paths where they affect tool-call speed or reliability.
- Specs, tests, and docs needed to make the speed/reliability contract durable.

## User Outcomes

- ADV feels faster during normal agent workflows.
- Common status and change-orientation calls avoid unnecessary fanout.
- Fast test commands return evidence promptly without hiding command results behind backend latency.
- Latency claims are backed by a live Temporal benchmark path, not only disk substitutes.
- Reliability is not traded away for speed: stale diagnostics, cache shortcuts, and best-effort recording failures are surfaced honestly.
- Users can distinguish authoritative state from cached diagnostic data.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Investigating and addressing all 15 Tron findings from the prior reconnaissance.
- Optimizing `adv_status` summary behavior and status-backed hydration hot paths.
- Reviewing `changes.listSummary`, `store.status()`, memo/cache/Visibility behavior, and cold-path hydration impact.
- Bounding or degrading non-authoritative status probes without treating stale diagnostics as safety proof.
- Improving `adv_run_test` evidence recording so command results are not lost to best-effort Temporal signal latency.
- Adding or enabling a live Temporal latency benchmark mode for authoritative performance evidence.
- Updating relevant specs for durable speed/reliability contracts when behavior changes.
- Adding tests for latency-sensitive behavior, timeout semantics, cache correctness, and degraded evidence reporting.
- Documentation updates for new benchmark or diagnostic behavior.

### Out of Scope

- Replacing Temporal as the ADV state backend.
- Broad rewrite of ADV storage or workflow architecture beyond what the findings require.
- Removing durable evidence recording, cache invalidation, or workflow-state correctness to reduce latency.
- Optimizing unrelated commands or subsystems that were not implicated by the Tron findings.
- Changing GitHub Project WSJF/value ownership or backlog ranking policy.
- Building a background daemon, new database layer, or separate performance service.

### Must Not

- Must not use stale cached probe data as authority for safety-critical recovery, archive, unlock, override, or mutation decisions.
- Must not bypass `fireSignalAndRefresh` / cache-invalidation discipline for speed on change-workflow mutations.
- Must not silently drop `adv_run_test` evidence recording; any recording degradation must be explicit and bounded.
- Must not make correctness depend on heuristics such as title/path inference when typed state, schemas, Visibility attributes, or tests can own the invariant.
- Must not make `adv_status` faster by hiding active changes, worktree hazards, or relevant health failures without a clear view/freshness boundary.
- Must not claim performance wins without benchmark or telemetry evidence.

## Affected Code

Likely areas, based on the read-only Tron evidence:

- `plugin/src/tools/status.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/probe-cache.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/test.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/backlog.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/roadmap.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts`
- `plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts`
- `plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/retry-wrapper.ts`
- `plugin/src/perf/latency.ts`
- `plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts`
- Relevant specs under `.adv/specs/`, especially `advance-meta`, `advance-workflow`, and `backlog-coordination` if behavior changes.
- Relevant docs/command guidance for status, diagnostics, benchmark usage, or test evidence semantics.

## Related Repositories

Current repository only: `advance`. No product-linked multi-repo scope is configured in `project.json`.

## Constraints

- Runtime is Bun; tests run on Node/Vitest.
- Runtime state is Temporal-only; disk projections are not authoritative for active workflow state.
- Zod schemas, Temporal search attributes, and typed workflow state should own correctness boundaries.
- Existing specs are law; affected spec requirements must be preserved or intentionally updated through normal ADV deltas.
- Local test routing should use repo-provided throttling where applicable.
- The change must preserve workflow replay safety and signal/query-only change-workflow mutation surface.

## Impact

Expected positive impact:

- Lower latency for common agent orientation and TDD hot paths.
- Better latency evidence and telemetry-driven optimization.
- Fewer tool wrapper timeouts on status/health surfaces.
- Clearer degraded states when best-effort or diagnostic work cannot complete.

Potential risk:

- Over-optimizing status could hide relevant state if summary/full-health boundaries are not explicit.
- Bounded `adv_run_test` recording could create evidence consistency gaps if degradation is not structurally reported.
- Probe cancellation changes could alter diagnostics behavior if not tested against stale/fresh cache cases.
- Live Temporal benchmarks can be misleading if setup requirements and mode labels are not strict.

## Context

Pre-flight `adv_status view:"summary"` timed out at the 10s safe-execute boundary during proposal setup, directly reinforcing the status-latency hotspot. `adv_change_list` remained responsive, so ADV state access is partially healthy and usable.

Relevant existing backlog/context:

- Pending agenda already includes `Optimize adv-status for large WIP sets`.
- Active draft `enforcePreviewVerification` is adjacent to evidence quality but not the same scope.
- Specs reviewed for likely overlap: `advance-meta`, `advance-workflow`, `backlog-coordination`.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Result | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Skills Considered | PASS | `adv-tron` used for reconnaissance; `adv-opportunity-scout` run through `adv-researcher`; no pending review skill found. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | Extended `docs/repo-improve-prep.md` and `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md`; added live evidence from current source and Temporal docs. |
| Conflict Scan | DEGRADED PASS | `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` and `adv_change_validate` timed out at 10s; active-list and agenda reads showed overlapping status/latency items. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases recorded for status, probes, test evidence recording, Visibility fallback, and benchmarks. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Questions classified with trust model, blast radius, alternatives. |
| Draft Spec Deltas | PASS | Draft rq-* shapes listed below. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Found status, backlog, test, metrics, latency, and probe-cache patterns. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Visibility-first, lazy view execution, typed degraded states, and probe caching align with current stack; replacing Temporal/Zod rejected. |
| Completeness Verification | PASS | Problem spans multiple tool-read and evidence paths; secondary surfaces classified below. |

### Skills Considered

- `adv-tron`: matched codebase reconnaissance; used to produce source-backed 15-finding scan.
- `adv-opportunity-scout`: matched broad architecture/performance discovery; scout found sequencing opportunities and one user tradeoff.
- No global skill with actual pending-review frontmatter was found. A textual example of `review_status: "pending"` in `adv-skill-author/SKILL.md` was not a pending skill.
- No new skill required; this is ADV-internal performance work covered by existing skills and repo research packs.

### Extends

- `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`: already identified lazy `adv_status`, status cleanup deferral, perf budgets, latency telemetry, summary-index read model, Visibility attribute drift, and rejection of Temporal replacement. New finding: status summary timeout reproduced during this proposal preflight, and current source confirms `adv_status` still begins with `loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry` before view-specific work (`plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1531-1535`).
- `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md`: earlier research identified shell/subprocess, structured classification, observability, and evidence-ledger concerns. New finding: current `adv_run_test` now uses streaming `spawn` and typed classification, but still silently catches best-effort `testRunRecordedSignal` failure (`plugin/src/tools/test.ts:440-477`).
- No `temp/*.md` brainstorm artifacts found.

### Conflict Scan

- Related active/agenda work: `ag-Pu9AaBst Optimize adv-status for large WIP sets` directly overlaps and should be absorbed or closed after this change completes.
- Related draft `enforcePreviewVerification` overlaps evidence quality only; not a conflict.
- `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` timed out, so archived-change conflict coverage is degraded. This itself is supporting evidence for the status/list performance problem.
- `adv_change_validate optimizeAdvToolCalls` timed out, so validation coverage is degraded and must be rerun during design/prep when read paths are healthier.

### Current State

- `adv_status` loads status through `loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry` before most view-specific logic (`plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1531-1535`).
- Recent-change enrichment is sequential (`plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1686-1706`).
- Status probes use `createProbeCache`, which provides an `AbortSignal` to fetchers (`plugin/src/tools/probe-cache.ts:14-24`, `plugin/src/tools/probe-cache.ts:73-78`, `plugin/src/tools/probe-cache.ts:94-102`). Status fetch closures do not thread the signal into `getTemporalHealth`, `getWorktreeCensus`, or `scanSnapshotHealth` (`plugin/src/tools/status.ts:200-211`, `plugin/src/tools/status.ts:241-245`).
- `changes.listSummary` exists as a lightweight summary surface, but its type explicitly says safety-critical callers must keep using authoritative `list`/`get` (`plugin/src/storage/store-types.ts:212-223`).
- `adv_change_list` already uses `listSummary` when available (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts:2183-2195`).
- `store.status()` still uses a full change resolution path that unions memo, Visibility, disk, and archive IDs, then hydrates in batches (`plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts:691-765`, `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts:803-858`).
- `adv_backlog_state` uses O(1) active-change annotation through `queryActiveChangesByIssueNumbers` (`plugin/src/tools/backlog.ts:395-402`); `adv_roadmap` has a Visibility-first path with slower fallback (`plugin/src/tools/roadmap.ts:743-867`).
- `adv_run_test` runs commands with streaming `spawn`, bounded output, timeout, process-group kill, typed classification, and phase telemetry (`plugin/src/tools/test.ts:189-310`, `plugin/src/tools/test.ts:431-438`, `plugin/src/tools/test.ts:496-519`).
- After command completion, `adv_run_test` awaits best-effort Temporal signal recording and catches failures silently (`plugin/src/tools/test.ts:440-477`).
- Metrics already record per-tool durations and recent phase durations in memory (`plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts:33-42`, `plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts:137-156`, `plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts:194-218`).
- The latency benchmark documents that disk mode does not measure Temporal RTT and currently refuses `--mode temporal` (`plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts:22-26`, `plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts:92-100`).
- Temporal TypeScript docs confirm workflow handles expose `signal`, `query`, and `describe`; query uses the `queryWorkflow` RPC; typed search attributes support Visibility read models.

### Edge Cases

- Status summary:
  - Large WIP set with many cold summary misses must not hydrate everything for default orientation.
  - Temporal/Visibility unavailable must fail or degrade explicitly, not render stale disk rows as live.
  - Health/hygiene-only failures must not block summary when default summary does not need that data.
- Probe cancellation/cache:
  - Stale cached probe returned after timeout must be labeled stale and must not authorize recovery actions.
  - Underlying APIs that cannot accept AbortSignal must be separately bounded or excluded from summary.
- `adv_run_test` evidence recording:
  - Command exits quickly but signal recording stalls; command result must not be lost behind wrapper timeout.
  - Command fails and recording also fails; output must distinguish command classification from recording status.
  - Task/change workflow absent or poisoned; recording degradation must be explicit and non-misleading.
- Visibility/listing:
  - `AdvProjectId` / `AdvAffectedProjects` drift can make fast paths unreliable and force slow fallback.
  - Cold start with memo empty must still discover legitimate open changes.
- Benchmarking:
  - Live Temporal worker missing must fail closed with setup remediation, not silently use disk substitute.
  - Benchmark results must label runtime, mode, iterations, warmup, and fixture/live project shape to avoid false claims.

### Open Design Questions

1. Lean summary field boundary.
   - Trust model: user-owned outcome boundary, agent designs details.
   - Blast radius: if too lean, users miss important hazards; if too broad, status remains slow.
   - User resolution: `Lean default (Recommended)` — fast orientation; health/hygiene detail moves to explicit views.
2. Latency posture.
   - Trust model: user-owned priority.
   - Blast radius: aggressive budgets may destabilize tests/scope; loose budgets may not fix pain.
   - User resolution: `Balanced (Recommended)` — realistic p95 budgets without flaky micro-optimization.
3. `adv_run_test` recording behavior.
   - Trust model: joint; user deferred exact behavior to design.
   - Blast radius: prompt return can weaken durability if poorly represented; waiting can hide fast command evidence behind backend latency.
   - Resolution: design must investigate and choose a structural policy; agreement constrains it to no silent recording failure and no hidden command result.
4. Visibility attribute alignment.
   - Trust model: agent-owned technical decision.
   - Blast radius: fast summary/index work built on drifted attributes can be flaky or slow.
   - Agent decision: fix or verify attribute alignment before relying on a Visibility-backed summary index.
5. Live benchmark mode.
   - Trust model: agent-owned technical decision with user-visible evidence.
   - Blast radius: misleading benchmark mode can create false performance claims.
   - Agent decision: live Temporal benchmark must fail closed when runtime prerequisites are absent and must not masquerade as disk mode.

### Draft Spec Deltas

- `rq-statusSummaryLazy01` (`advance-meta`): `adv_status view:"summary"` must execute a summary-specific plan that omits health/hygiene-only probes and expensive cleanup by default while preserving active/recent orientation and explicit degradation/freshness markers.
  - Given a summary status request, when health/hygiene detail is not required, then health/hygiene-only probes are not executed and summary output still includes active/recent orientation.
- `rq-probeAbortSignal01` (`advance-meta`): cached status probes that advertise a timeout/AbortSignal must either thread cancellation into the underlying operation or explicitly classify the operation as non-cancellable and bounded elsewhere.
  - Given a probe timeout, when stale data is returned, then freshness metadata includes `stale:true` and the stale value is diagnostic-only.
- `rq-advRunTestRecordingLatency01` (`advance-meta` or `tdd-contract`): `adv_run_test` command-result output must not be lost to best-effort recording latency; recording failures must be explicit, bounded, and non-silent.
  - Given a command has completed and evidence recording fails or times out, when output is returned, then command classification and recording status are both observable.
- `rq-liveLatencyBenchmark01` (`advance-meta`): latency benchmark tooling must distinguish disk substitute from live Temporal mode and fail closed when live prerequisites are absent.
  - Given `--mode temporal` without a live Temporal bundle, when benchmark starts, then it exits with setup remediation and does not emit disk-substitute numbers as live results.
- `rq-visibilityProjectScope01` (`backlog-coordination` or `advance-meta`): project-scoped Visibility attributes used by fast read paths must be registered, consistent, and covered by tests before summary/index paths depend on them.
  - Given a project with open changes, when the fast read path lists them by project scope, then it uses the registered project-scope attribute consistently and does not fall back due to attribute-name drift.

### Related Pattern Scan

- Status/read-model patterns: `status.ts`, `store-temporal/index.ts`, `store-temporal/changes.ts`, `change.ts`.
- Probe/cache patterns: `probe-cache.ts`, `status.ts`, `temporal-ops.ts`.
- Test evidence patterns: `test.ts`, `subagent-report.ts`, `tdd-contract` references in tests/specs.
- Visibility annotation patterns: `backlog.ts`, `roadmap.ts`, `visibility-claim-queries.ts`.
- Metrics/benchmark patterns: `metrics.ts`, `safe-execute.ts`, `perf/latency.ts`, `bench-adv-latency.ts`.
- Deprecated/archived ACP experiment not examined; out of supported package scope.

### Completeness Verification

- Problem-completeness: high confidence. Discovery covers the whole observed problem class: slow read/orientation calls, slow or hidden test-evidence recording, stale/cached diagnostic semantics, and missing live benchmark proof. The timed-out `adv_status`/validation/list calls show the issue is not only one source file.
- Solution-scope: medium-high confidence. The intended solution is not a single implementation piece; it includes lazy status execution, summary/index reliability, probe bounds, test recording semantics, benchmark evidence, and spec/test safeguards.
- Sole-entry claim: none relied on. Discovery does not claim one chokepoint for all tool latency; surfaces are classified separately.
- Secondary surfaces:
  - `adv_change_show` lightweight mode: out of scope with rationale; noted in prep pack but not among the highest-priority Tron findings for this change.
  - `adv_task_list`, `adv_wip_state`, `adv_backlog_state`: out of scope unless benchmark evidence during design shows they dominate the same hot path.
  - CLI `/adv-status` and `bin/adv status`: in scope only if source changes affect shared status read-model semantics; no new CLI command surface promised.
  - `adv_roadmap`: in scope only to preserve/fix Visibility-first annotation fallback if touched; `adv_backlog_state` is preferred agent read path.

### LBP Check

- Keep Temporal. External docs and existing specs support Temporal as durable workflow runtime; replacing it is out of scope and not justified for latency alone.
- Prefer Visibility/search-attribute read models for fast, worker-free summaries when state is already projected there; use workflow queries for authoritative full state and safety-critical operations.
- Prefer requested-view execution planning over build-everything-then-project. This matches repo prep findings and avoids hidden hot-path work.
- Prefer typed degraded states over silent best-effort catches. This preserves reliability while allowing bounded latency.
- Preserve Zod/tool-schema validation; no evidence shows validation is the bottleneck, and it owns correctness at boundaries.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

- Trigger decision: run — broad architecture/performance change with sequencing risk.
- Candidates considered: 5.
- Auto-adopted: 4.
  - Split status optimization into low-risk lazy probe/view gating before higher-risk summary-index/hydration changes.
  - Treat Visibility attribute alignment as prerequisite for fast read-model dependence.
  - Thread/categorize probe AbortSignal behavior early because it is low-risk and supports timeout reliability.
  - Make `adv_run_test` recording failure structural and non-silent.
- Surfaced to user: 1.
  - Lean summary field boundary; user selected lean default.
- Degradation/repair: scout worker created 4 agenda items despite read-only workflow; orchestrator cancelled them immediately as unauthorized workflow side effects and retained them only as suggestions in this discovery text.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C

No CRITICAL findings.

- M1 HIGH Missing Information: exact `adv_run_test` slow-recording behavior deferred to design.
  - Evidence: User answer to `Test evidence` question: `investigate and defer to design`.
  - Reason: exact return-vs-wait-vs-fail behavior changes contract semantics and needs design proof.
  - Disposition: continue; agreement constrains design with no silent recording failure and no hidden command result.
- S1 LOW Completion Signals: balanced latency budgets need final benchmark fixture definitions.
  - Evidence: Proposal asked for `Target latency SLOs for common tools`.
  - Reason: acceptance budgets require fixture/live mode labeling to avoid flaky claims.
  - Disposition: include balanced p95 targets and benchmark-report requirements in agreement; design may refine fixture mechanics without weakening targets.

## Discovery Agenda

Resolved during discovery:

- Status summary should optimize for lean default behavior.
- Performance posture is balanced.
- `adv_run_test` exact recording behavior is deferred to design, with non-silent/degraded-state constraints.
- Replacing Temporal and removing Zod validation are rejected.
- Live Temporal benchmark needs fail-closed mode labeling.

Carried into design:

- Exact status summary field contract.
- Exact `adv_run_test` degraded-recording output schema.
- Visibility attribute alignment/migration strategy.
- Benchmark fixture mechanics and threshold enforcement.

We need optimize these paths without weakening reliability guarantees: Temporal correctness, cache invalidation, diagnostic safety, durable evidence, and spec-law behavior must remain structural and verified.

# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Optimize common ADV tool-call paths without weakening Temporal/state/evidence reliability.
2. Make `adv_status view:"summary"` a lean default orientation surface, not a health/hygiene archaeology surface.
3. Preserve detailed diagnostics in explicit `health` and `hygiene` views.
4. Ensure status/list fast paths use summary/Visibility data only where that response contract is sufficient.
5. Ensure `adv_run_test` command results are not hidden by slow or failed evidence recording.
6. Provide benchmark/telemetry evidence for performance claims, including live Temporal mode or fail-closed remediation.
7. Dispose all 15 Tron findings explicitly before acceptance.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: `adv_status view:"summary"` is lean by default: fast orientation, no health/hygiene archaeology unless explicitly requested.
- SC2: Hot-path latency improves with balanced p95 targets: `adv_status summary` p95 ≤ 2s on live current project state; `adv_change_list` default p95 ≤ 1s on live current project state; `adv_run_test` fast-command overhead after command exit p95 ≤ 500ms, excluding command runtime, under designed recording policy.
- SC3: All 15 Tron findings have explicit final disposition: fixed, rejected with evidence, or intentionally deferred with rationale.
- SC4: Reliability remains structural: no stale diagnostic data authorizes safety-critical mutations.
- SC5: Performance claims are backed by benchmark/telemetry evidence, including live Temporal mode or fail-closed remediation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: `adv_status view:"summary"` skips health/hygiene-only probes and cleanup work by default while preserving active/recent orientation and degraded/freshness markers. [warrant: tool:adv_status#view]
- AC2: `adv_status view:"health"` and `view:"hygiene"` retain detailed diagnostics that summary omits. [warrant: tool:adv_status#view]
- AC3: Status probe timeout behavior is bounded and tested; stale probe output remains diagnostic-only, never mutation authority. [warrant: spec:rq-statusProbeCache01]
- AC4: `changes.listSummary` / Visibility fast paths are used only where their response contract is sufficient; authoritative gates/recovery/archive paths still use full state. [warrant: spec:rq-statusCliWorkerFree01]
- AC5: `adv_run_test` no longer silently swallows evidence-recording failure; design-approved output exposes command result and recording status distinctly. [warrant: tool:adv_run_test]
- AC6: Live Temporal latency benchmark mode exists or fails closed with setup remediation; disk-substitute results are never labeled live.
- AC7: Tests cover large-WIP/default-summary behavior, probe stale/timeout behavior, `adv_run_test` slow/failing recording behavior, and benchmark mode labeling.
- AC8: Docs/specs updated where behavior contracts change; existing schema/Temporal replay safety checks remain green.

## Constraints

- C1: Runtime is Bun; tests run on Node/Vitest.
- C2: Runtime state is Temporal-only; disk projections are not authoritative for active workflow state.
- C3: Zod schemas, Temporal search attributes, typed workflow state, and tests own correctness boundaries.
- C4: Existing specs are law; affected requirements must be preserved or intentionally updated through ADV deltas.
- C5: Preserve workflow replay safety and the signal/query-only change-workflow mutation surface.
- C6: Use repo test throttle policy where applicable.
- C7: Summary fast paths may not become safety authority for gates, archive, recovery, or mutation decisions.

## Avoidances

- DONT1: Do not replace Temporal as the ADV state backend.
- DONT2: Do not broadly rewrite ADV storage/workflow architecture beyond what findings require.
- DONT3: Do not remove durable evidence recording, cache invalidation, or workflow-state correctness for speed.
- DONT4: Do not optimize unrelated commands/subsystems not implicated by Tron findings.
- DONT5: Do not use stale cached probe data as authority for safety-critical recovery, archive, unlock, override, or mutation decisions.
- DONT6: Do not bypass `fireSignalAndRefresh` / cache-invalidation discipline for change-workflow mutations.
- DONT7: Do not silently drop `adv_run_test` evidence recording; any recording degradation must be explicit and bounded.
- DONT8: Do not make correctness depend on title/path heuristics where typed state, schemas, Visibility attributes, or tests can own the invariant.
- DONT9: Do not claim performance wins without benchmark or telemetry evidence.

## Out of Scope

- OOS1: Replacing Temporal.
- OOS2: Building a background daemon, new database layer, or separate performance service.
- OOS3: Changing GitHub Project WSJF/value ownership or backlog ranking policy.
- OOS4: `adv_change_show` lightweight mode unless design proves it is required for agreed latency targets.
- OOS5: Optimizing `adv_task_list`, `adv_wip_state`, or `adv_backlog_state` unless benchmark evidence shows they dominate the same hot path.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: false

Rationale: This change affects ADV tool behavior, diagnostics, benchmarks, and docs/specs. It does not change browser-visible UI or visual output.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

- Latency posture: Balanced. Acceptance should use realistic p95 targets without flaky micro-optimization.
- Status summary boundary: Lean default. Summary should provide fast orientation with explicit freshness/degraded markers; health/hygiene detail belongs in explicit views.
- `adv_run_test` recording behavior: User deferred exact policy to design. Design must investigate and choose a structural policy while preserving no-silent-failure and no-hidden-command-result constraints.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

- Keep Temporal. External Temporal TypeScript docs and existing specs support durable workflow state; replacement is out of scope.
- Prefer Visibility/search-attribute read models for fast summaries where already projected; use workflow queries/full state for authoritative operations.
- Prefer requested-view execution planning over build-everything-then-project.
- Prefer typed degraded states over silent best-effort catches.
- Preserve Zod/tool-schema validation; no evidence shows validation is bottleneck.
- Treat Visibility attribute alignment as a prerequisite before relying on a Visibility-backed summary/index fast path.

## Deferred Questions

- Exact `adv_run_test` degraded-recording output schema and wait/fail/return policy are deferred to design, constrained by AC5 and DONT7.
- Exact benchmark fixture mechanics are deferred to design, constrained by SC2, SC5, and AC6.
- Exact status summary field list is deferred to design, constrained by the user's Lean default decision and AC1/AC2.

## Sign-Off

User approved criteria and agreement via chat reply: `approve`.

# Design

## Architecture Overview

Optimize hot ADV tool calls through view-specific read planning, typed degraded states, and benchmark-backed validation. Temporal remains authoritative. Cached, summary, and Visibility projections are allowed only for explicitly lightweight read contracts.

Design slices:

1. **Status summary residuals** — keep existing plan-gating scaffold and remove remaining summary hot spots: unguarded full status load, serial enrichment, and hygiene/cleanup work not needed for lean orientation.
2. **Summary read-model discipline** — `changes.listSummary` and Visibility metadata power lightweight orientation; full workflow state remains mandatory for gates, archive, recovery, task mutation, contracts, acceptance, and release proof.
3. **Probe timeout semantics** — `probe-cache.ts` already threads `AbortSignal` into `options.fetch`; fix target is status probe closures that drop `context.signal`, plus explicit non-cancellable classification for probes that cannot honor signal.
4. **`adv_run_test` recording status** — command execution result and evidence-recording result are distinct; recording failures become bounded typed degradation, not silent catches.
5. **Latency evidence harness** — benchmark/telemetry reports distinguish disk substitute from live Temporal mode; live mode fails closed without prerequisites.
6. **Spec-backed behavior** — status/probe/test/benchmark behavior changes receive spec deltas and tests.

## Key Decisions

### KD1 — Finish status summary residuals instead of rewriting status

Keep existing plan-gated status providers where they work. Target remaining summary hot spots:

- `loadStatusWithBootstrapRetry(activeStore)` still runs before view-specific logic (`plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1531-1535`).
- Recent-change enrichment is serial (`plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1686-1706`).
- Summary still risks paying for health/hygiene-only providers identified by prior prep (`docs/repo-improve-prep.md:35-47`).

Lean summary field boundary:

- include active/recent orientation, counts, current project/config warnings, bounded recommendations, degraded/freshness markers, and pointers to `health`/`hygiene` for omitted detail;
- exclude snapshot-health scan, archive leak archaeology, worktree cleanup retry/discovery, detailed queue/search verification, peer-session archaeology, and detailed session debt unless `view:"health"` or `view:"hygiene"`.

### KD2 — Summary projections are non-authoritative

`changes.listSummary` / Visibility projections may feed orientation and benchmarkable default reads only. Store type already says safety-critical callers must keep using `list` / `get` when authoritative workflow state is required (`plugin/src/storage/store-types.ts:212-223`).

Full state remains required for gate completion, archive/release, recovery/repair, task mutation, contract mint/review, acceptance proof, and release proof.

### KD3 — Visibility project-scope drift is verification/cleanup

Design scout found the suspected `AdvProjectId` / `AdvAffectedProjects` issue is likely stale/dead constant cleanup rather than a gate-blocking prerequisite. Prep must audit current registration/query usage and remove/quarantine stale `AdvProjectId` references if dead; migrate only if tests prove a live inconsistency.

### KD4 — Probe cancellation gap is in status closures

`createProbeCache` already passes `{ signal, staleValue }` into `options.fetch` and uses stale-on-abort settings. `lru-cache` docs confirm `cache.fetch(key, { signal })`, fetch coalescing, and stale-on-abort behavior; examples thread `signal` into the fetch body.

Fix target: status probe closures at `plugin/src/tools/status.ts:200-211` and `plugin/src/tools/status.ts:241-245` do not forward `context.signal` into underlying operations. Where underlying operations cannot honor signal, classify as non-cancellable and keep out of summary or bound at adapter level.

### KD5 — `adv_run_test` exposes recording status separately

After command completion, `adv_run_test` attempts evidence recording within a bounded wait. Output includes command `classification`/`passed` plus recording status:

```ts
type TestEvidenceRecordingStatus =
  | { status: "recorded"; runId: string }
  | { status: "degraded"; runId: string; reason: "timeout" | "temporal_unavailable" | "workflow_unreachable" | "signal_failed"; message: string }
  | { status: "not_applicable"; reason: "no_change_for_task" | "service_unavailable" };
```

Policy:

- Command result returns even when recording degrades.
- Recording failures are not swallowed silently.
- Recording degradation does not turn completed command execution into command failure.
- Default post-command recording wait budget: ≤ 300ms for fast-command path.

Current motivation: `testRunRecordedSignal` is awaited and caught silently (`plugin/src/tools/test.ts:440-477`).

### KD6 — Live benchmark mode is fail-closed and labeled

Extend benchmark reports with `mode: disk | temporal`, runtime setup metadata, iterations/warmup, operation table, and environment labels. `--mode temporal` must require real Temporal-backed setup and fail with remediation if absent. Disk substitute may remain, but cannot be compared to live Temporal thresholds.

Current motivation: benchmark says disk mode does not measure Temporal RTT and currently refuses temporal mode (`plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts:22-26`, `plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts:92-100`).

### KD7 — Specs change with behavior

Likely spec homes:

- `advance-meta`: lean status summary, probe timeout/degraded semantics, latency benchmark labeling, `adv_run_test` recording-status latency surface.
- `tdd-contract`: only if `adv_run_test` changes TDD evidence semantics.
- `backlog-coordination`: only if Visibility project-scope behavior changes live code.

## ADR Drafts

No ADR draft required. Decisions are captured in design/spec deltas and are reversible through normal ADV change flow.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Spec deltas first** — add/update requirements for lean status summary, probe cancellation/degraded semantics, `adv_run_test` recording status, and live benchmark labeling.
2. **Probe semantics** — forward `context.signal` from status probe closures where possible; classify/bound non-cancellable probes; test fresh cache, stale-on-timeout, cold timeout, and diagnostic-only stale metadata.
3. **`adv_run_test` recording status** — add typed output union, bounded signal wait, explicit degradation tests, and preserve command result compatibility.
4. **Status summary residuals** — replace unguarded summary full-load path where safe, use/cap `changes.listSummary`, parallelize/cap emitted-row enrichment, and move cleanup/archive archaeology to hygiene/explicit cleanup surfaces.
5. **Visibility cleanup verification** — audit `AdvProjectId` / `AdvAffectedProjects`; remove stale constants or migrate only if live tests prove needed.
6. **Latency benchmark/evidence** — implement live/fail-closed mode labels and benchmark agreed hot paths: `adv_status summary`, `adv_change_list` default, and `adv_run_test` post-command overhead.
7. **Verification/disposition** — targeted tests + repo smoke/check; final table mapping all 15 Tron findings to fixed/rejected/deferred.

## LBP Analysis

- Keep Temporal; optimize round trips/read plans rather than replacing runtime.
- Use Visibility/search attributes for lightweight read models where projected state is sufficient.
- Prefer requested-view execution planning over build-everything-then-project.
- Prefer typed degraded states over silent best-effort catches.
- Preserve Zod/tool-schema validation; no evidence shows validation is bottleneck.
- Use schemas/tests/specs as correctness authority, not title/path heuristics.

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/tools/status.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/probe-cache.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/test.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/backlog.ts`, `plugin/src/tools/roadmap.ts` if Visibility fallback/annotation touched
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-types.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/*search*`, `visibility-claim-queries.ts`, `list-change-workflows.ts`
- `plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts`, `plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts`
- `plugin/src/perf/latency.ts`, `plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts`
- `.adv/specs/*`, `docs/specs/*`, related asset tests

## Design-Derived Criteria

DDC1. Summary status provider plan must not invoke snapshot-health scan, worktree cleanup retry/discovery, archive leak archaeology, or full status hydration unless tests prove the call is bounded and required for AC1.

DDC2. New recording-status output must be a typed union with stable string enum values and tests for every status.

DDC3. Default `adv_run_test` post-command recording wait budget is ≤ 300ms for fast-command path.

DDC4. Benchmark reports must include operation name, mode, iterations, warmup, min/p50/p95/max/avg, and environment/runtime labels.

DDC5. No benchmark assertion may compare disk-substitute numbers against live Temporal thresholds.

DDC6. Every Tron finding must map to a final disposition row before acceptance.

DDC7. If `AdvProjectId` is stale/dead, cleanup must remove or quarantine it with tests rather than building new logic around it.

## Risks / Mitigations

- Lean summary hides useful warnings → keep degraded/freshness markers and route detail to `health`/`hygiene`.
- Summary read model omits authoritative state → typed boundary; safety-critical paths keep full state.
- Visibility cleanup assumption wrong → audit first; migrate only if tests prove inconsistency.
- Recording degradation weakens TDD proof → explicit status, bounded wait, tests, review-matrix evidence.
- Benchmarks flaky → separate deterministic budget tests from live Temporal evidence; label modes strictly.
- Probe cannot cancel underlying API → classify non-cancellable and exclude from summary or bound adapter-level timeout.

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout result: VALIDATED with two corrections incorporated.

- Adopted: probe signal-threading target narrowed to status closures; `adv_run_test` recording-status field; targeted status residual work; KD3 downgraded from prerequisite migration to verification/cleanup.
- Surfaced tradeoff: summary mandatory-field boundary. User had already selected Lean default; field boundary codified in KD1.
- Note: scout auto-created follow-up agenda items despite read-only intent; design retains findings in artifact and treats agenda side effects as unauthorized workflow noise.

## Validator Result

Validator verdict: VALIDATED.

Findings:

- Correctness: design solves objectives and AC1–AC8 map to verified code/spec anchors.
- Simplicity: design uses residual cleanup, existing lru-cache stale-while-revalidate, and fail-closed benchmark mode; no simpler equivalent overlooked.
- Spec-law: no conflict with `rq-statusProbeCache01`, `rq-statusCliWorkerFree01`, `rq-dashboardWorkerFree01`, or `rq-backlogCoord05`.
- Caution resolved in this revision: previous KD4 prose could imply `probe-cache.ts` failed to thread signal. Corrected: `probe-cache.ts` is sound; status probe closures are the gap.

Recommendation: proceed to planning.

# Executive Summary

## Outcome
ADV tool-call hot paths were optimized without weakening Temporal/state correctness. The default status surface is leaner, evidence recording failures are explicit and bounded, Visibility project scope uses registered attributes, and benchmark output clearly separates disk substitute from live Temporal evidence.

## Verdict
APPROVED

## What Was Built
1. Updated speed/reliability specs and docs for lean status summary, probe semantics, benchmark labeling, and `adv_run_test` recording status.
2. Threaded abort/cancellation signals through status probe providers and preserved stale probe output as diagnostics only.
3. Added typed `adv_run_test.evidenceRecording` states: `recorded`, `degraded`, and `not_applicable`; bounded recording wait to 300ms.
4. Aligned Visibility project-scope reads on registered `AdvAffectedProjects` and removed stale `AdvProjectId` export/docs drift.
5. Removed archived-branch hygiene work from default `adv_status view:"summary"`; detail remains in hygiene/health views.
6. Improved latency benchmark fixture/reporting so disk mode is labeled, isolated, and exercises `adv_run_test`; temporal mode fails closed with remediation and no disk substitution.
7. Completed final contract verification and 15 finding dispositions.

## What Was Verified
- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 blockers, 0 issues, 0 suggestions, 0 nits; positive reviewer note for bounded evidence-recording remediation.
- Tests: targeted regression suite passed (128 tests); reviewer targeted suites passed (111 tests plus workflow boundary/search-attr tests); `schemas:check` passed; `typecheck` passed.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — agreement declares `visual_surface: false`; implementation is ADV tool/runtime/docs behavior with no browser-visible UI or visual output.
- Contract matrix: 34 rows persisted; required rows passed/respected/not_applicable; 0 failing/violated/unknown rows.

## Remaining Concerns
None. One unrelated pre-existing deploy-local prompt ceiling failure remains outside this change scope and was not touched.
